name: GitHub Advanced Security — GH-500
summary: GitHub & Microsoft's specialty credential for securing the software development lifecycle with GitHub Advanced Security. Validates that you can stand up and operate Secret Protection (secret scanning + push protection), Supply Chain Security (dependency graph, Dependabot, Dependency Review, SBOMs), and Code Security (CodeQL code scanning + SARIF), run security operations end to end — triage, prioritize with severity/EPSS, and remediate at scale with campaigns and rulesets — and administer it all across repository, organization, and enterprise with security configurations, roles, policies, and automation.

exam.recommendedPrep: MS Learn GitHub Advanced Security paths Parts 1 & 2 + hands-on GHAS on a real repo

blueprintNote: Domains and weights are normalized from the official GH-500 skills-measured ranges (Security suites & ecosystem 15–20%, Secret Protection 15–20%, Supply Chain Security 15–20%, Code Security 10–15%, Security operations 15–20%, Administration 10–15%), each taken inside its band so the six sum to exactly 1.0 — the engine reads weights from data, so a correction is a content edit. Format is modeled as ~60 scored questions, 100 minutes, 700/1000 to pass, proctored via Pearson VUE. The 100-minute duration and 700/1000 passing score ARE stated on the official pages; the ~60 question count is the GitHub/Microsoft exam-family norm and is NOT stated on the official study guide or certification page — flagged, confirm before launch. The scenario drills are an Academy training device: neither official page describes a scenario-based or case-study question format, so scenarios are not represented as an official GH-500 question type.

verification.notes:
Canonical study-guide URL is the 301 target of https://aka.ms/GH500-StudyGuide. Live check 2026-07-02 against both official pages (study guide 'Skills measured as of July 2026', ms.date 2026-04-26 / updated_at 2026-05-14; cert page Last Updated 05/04/2026). Confirmed: six domains with ranges D1 15–20% / D2 15–20% / D3 15–20% / D4 10–15% / D5 15–20% / D6 10–15%; 100 minutes; 700 to pass; proctored via Pearson VUE; Intermediate level; roles Administrator/Developer/DevOps Engineer/Solution Architect/Student; languages English, Spanish, Portuguese (BR), Korean, Japanese; exam maintained by GitHub, delivered by Microsoft; change log states the exam changed significantly on July 2026. Neither page states a question count. Neither page describes a scenario/case-study question structure — cert page says only 'You may have interactive components to complete as part of this exam' and the sandbox lets you 'interact with different question types'. A practice assessment exists (assessmentId 590484996) but is an interactive gated tool, not an exam-details page.

DRIFT vs PRD-S0-GH500.md sections 2-3 (verbatim titles / no correction to weights or exam facts needed):

1. Domain TITLES in PRD table (§3) are paraphrased and drop the official "(formerly ...)" parentheticals. Use these VERBATIM official titles for currency-trap fidelity:
   - d4: PRD has "Configure and use Code Security (CodeQL)"; official is "Configure and use Code Security (formerly Code Scanning with CodeQL)". PRD substituted "(CodeQL)" for the official "(formerly Code Scanning with CodeQL)".
   - d5: PRD has "Security operations — prioritization & remediation"; official is "Security operations: best practices, prioritization, and remediation" (PRD dropped "best practices"; colon not em-dash).
   - d2: official "Configure and use Secret Protection (formerly secret scanning)" (PRD dropped the parenthetical).
   - d3: official "Configure and use supply chain security (formerly Dependabot/Dependency Review)" — note official is lowercase "supply chain security" (PRD title-cased it).
   - d1 and d6 titles match official wording (minus casing).

2. WEIGHTS: no correction. All six PRD weights sit INSIDE their official ranges and sum to 1.00 exactly — D1 0.18 in [0.15,0.20]; D2 0.18 in [0.15,0.20]; D3 0.18 in [0.15,0.20]; D4 0.13 in [0.10,0.15]; D5 0.18 in [0.15,0.20]; D6 0.15 in [0.10,0.15] (upper-boundary, inclusive). PRD's "Sum = 1.00, within range" claim is verified.

3. EXAM FACTS: PRD §2 states 100 min / 700 to pass / 1000 scale / proctored Pearson VUE / Intermediate / roles (admin, developer, DevOps, solution architect) / languages (English, Spanish, Portuguese BR, Korean, Japanese) — ALL confirmed by the live pages. Note the cert page ALSO lists "Student" as a role (PRD omits Student). The 1000 scale is not restated on either page but is standard MS scoring and consistent with the "700 to pass" that IS stated.

4. QUESTION COUNT: PRD §2/§3 hardcodes questionCount 60 in track.exam{} while §2 flags it as an assumption ("not stated on the guide — assume ~60 scored; confirm on the cert page before launch"). Confirmed: question count is NOT stated on either the study guide or the cert page. The '60' remains an unverified assumption — flag before launch; the official pages do not support hardcoding it.

5. SCENARIO/CASE-STUDY: neither official page describes a scenario-based or case-study question STRUCTURE. Cert page says only "You may have interactive components to complete as part of this exam" and the sandbox lets you "interact with different question types" — generic, not a named case-study format. PRD §4's "1-2 branching scenarios" is authored-content design, not an official exam-format claim, so no conflict — but do not represent scenarios as an official GH-500 question type.

6. REFRESH NAMES current: YES. The 'Skills measured as of July 2026' guide uses Secret Protection / Supply Chain Security / Code Security as PRIMARY domain names with '(formerly ...)' parentheticals, and the change log confirms 'This exam has changed significantly ... on July, 2026.' PRD's currency-trap guidance (§2) is correct.

7. Study guide 'ms.date' 2026-04-26, updated_at 2026-05-14; cert page Last Updated 05/04/2026. Practice assessment exists (assessmentId 590484996) but is an interactive gated tool, not an exam-details page, and states no scored-question count.

badge.definition: Completed the Automatos Academy GH-500 preparation track to the A+ readiness standard: ≥90% blueprint-weighted mastery and a full-length mock passed with margin (≥800/1000). This certifies preparation completion — the GH-500 (GitHub Advanced Security) credential itself is issued only by GitHub/Microsoft.

officialResources.title: GH-500 Study Guide — official (Microsoft Learn)
officialResources.annotation: The authoritative blueprint: the six skills-measured domains ('as of July 2026'), their weight ranges, the audience profile, and the July-2026 change log. This curriculum is built directly to it — including the current Secret Protection / Supply Chain Security / Code Security names with their '(formerly …)' parentheticals.

officialResources.title: GitHub Advanced Security (GH-500) — certification page
officialResources.annotation: Exam overview: 100 minutes, proctored, Intermediate, roles (Administrator/Developer/DevOps/Solution Architect/Student), languages, Pearson VUE scheduling, exam sandbox, and the practice assessment. Notes the exam is maintained by GitHub and delivered by Microsoft.

officialResources.title: GitHub Advanced Security Part 1 of 2 (Microsoft Learn path)
officialResources.annotation: Official learning path (4 modules): Introduction to GHAS, Dependabot security updates, secret scanning, and code scanning with CodeQL/Actions. Grounds D1–D4. Work it end to end.

officialResources.title: GitHub Advanced Security Part 2 of 2 (Microsoft Learn path)
officialResources.annotation: Official learning path (4 modules): CodeQL codebase analysis, code scanning with CodeQL, GitHub administration for GHAS, and managing sensitive data & security policies. Grounds D4–D6 and the operations/administration domains.

officialResources.title: GitHub code security documentation (root)
officialResources.annotation: Primary source for every product claim on this track: secret scanning & push protection, supply chain (dependency graph, Dependabot, Dependency Review, SBOM), code scanning/CodeQL & SARIF, and security overview / configurations / campaigns. When a community guide and the docs disagree, the docs win.
